message:
bd-2wxz: Implement controlled failure narrative for demo_showcase

Add failure narrative types to support debugging demos:
- FailureScenario enum with 4 scenarios (DatabaseTimeout, ConfigValidation,
  HealthCheckFailure, ResourceExhaustion)
- FailureEvent struct with context and stack trace for debugging tools
- StackFrame for simulated traceback display
- Methods: trigger_failure(), generate_failure_logs(), generate_prefailure_logs()
- Constructors: with_failure_scenario(), demo_with_failure()
- 9 new tests covering all failure scenarios

Each scenario provides:
- Realistic error messages and context data
- Simulated stack traces with function names, files, and line numbers
- Log sequences showing the failure progression
- State updates to affected services and pipeline stages

This enables the Traceback scene (bd-3fqt) to show rich_rust's
debugging capabilities with realistic failure data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-11gq","title":"Tree node markup parsing tests","description":"## Problem
Tree nodes may have similar markup parsing issues. Need explicit tests to verify and document behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test Tree node labels with markup - verify if parsed
- [ ] Test Tree with styled branches
- [ ] Verify ANSI codes present in rendered output (if markup should work)
- [ ] Verify raw markup tags NOT present in rendered output
- [ ] Document the expected API clearly

## Implementation Notes
- Check Tree implementation for markup handling
- Add explicit tests to lock down current behavior
- May need Tree::add_node_markup() if markup isn't supported

## Files to modify
- tests/conformance/tree_tests.rs  
- src/renderables/tree.rs (if fix needed)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:52.753560616Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T08:00:16.526581194Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T08:00:16.526523125Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-11zc","title":"demo_showcase tests: e2e harness + timeout runner","description":"Build a reusable integration test harness that spawns `demo_showcase` with timeouts, captures output, and logs diagnostics.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedWaterfall","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:01.710821962Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:26:21.084119423Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:26:21.084057027Z","close_reason":"Implemented e2e test harness (DemoRunner, RunResult, timeout handling) and 15+ e2e test cases. All 29 tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-11zc","depends_on_id":"bd-1jk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:37.078658697Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-11zc","depends_on_id":"bd-2sk1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:01.752771403Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":66,"issue_id":"bd-11zc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Harness requirements

Implement a small test helper (Rust, in `tests/` or a shared module) that can:
- locate the compiled binary via `env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_demo_showcase")`
- run with specific args + env overrides
- capture stdout/stderr
- enforce a hard timeout (kill the process)
- log diagnostics using `test-log` + `tracing`

## Diagnostic logging

On every run log:
- full argv
- env overrides
- start/stop timestamps + duration
- exit status
- byte counts for stdout/stderr

On failure, additionally log:
- the first/last N lines of stdout/stderr (truncate to avoid CI spam)

## Acceptance criteria

- A single helper function makes it easy to write new e2e tests without duplication.
- Timeouts are enforced reliably (no hanging CI).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:49Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-12nv","title":"demo_showcase: non-interactive safety policy","description":"Specify TTY/non-TTY behavior rules and ensure no prompts/animations run when piped.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:18.059944267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:14:20.111288380Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:34.024208222Z","close_reason":"Completed (TTY/non-TTY safety policy specified in README demo_showcase section)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-12nv","depends_on_id":"bd-23h8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:35.899375451Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-12nv","depends_on_id":"bd-sowt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:18.075242767Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":18,"issue_id":"bd-12nv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Policy

Define hard rules so the demo is safe everywhere:

- If stdout is not a TTY and `--force-terminal` is not set:
  - disable live refresh
  - disable alt-screen
  - disable prompt/pager
  - print static snapshots only
- If interactive is disabled explicitly (`--no-interactive`):
  - skip prompts
  - do not rely on keypresses to proceed

## Acceptance criteria

- No scene may require user input to terminate.
- No infinite loops; all animation is time-bounded and/or gated on TTY.
- Output remains readable when ANSI is suppressed.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:46Z"},{"id":78,"issue_id":"bd-12nv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing implication

The non-interactive safety policy must be enforced not only manually but via automated tests (see `bd-zzss`).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:14:20Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1365","title":"demo_showcase: Pretty/Inspect scene","description":"Create a scene that demonstrates Pretty + Inspect on real structs used by the demo.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:47.747083708Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:24.765710064Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","pretty"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1365","depends_on_id":"bd-1388","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:20.024630819Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1365","depends_on_id":"bd-1by8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:22.439133588Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1365","depends_on_id":"bd-1jk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:24.765677032Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1365","depends_on_id":"bd-2xsi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:47.762578230Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":47,"issue_id":"bd-1365","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Content

Use `Pretty` and `Inspect` on *real demo structs* so the output feels practical:
- `Config`
- `DemoState` snapshot
- a fake API request struct

## Acceptance criteria

- Output is readable and styled (e.g., via panels) rather than raw `Debug`.
- Demonstrates both `Pretty::new(&value)` and `inspect(&console, &value)` where appropriate.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:44:28Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1365","title":"demo_showcase: Pretty/Inspect scene","description":"Create a scene that demonstrates Pretty + Inspect on real structs used by the demo.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedWaterfall","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:47.747083708Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:43:02.461928739Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:43:02.461868747Z","close_reason":"Pretty/Inspect scene implemented: demonstrates Pretty on ServiceInfo, Inspect on DemoStateSnapshot with field tables, and FailureEvent context inspection. 86 tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","pretty"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1365","depends_on_id":"bd-1388","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:20.024630819Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1365","depends_on_id":"bd-1by8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:22.439133588Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1365","depends_on_id":"bd-1jk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:24.765677032Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1365","depends_on_id":"bd-2xsi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:47.762578230Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":47,"issue_id":"bd-1365","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Content

- `Config`

## Acceptance criteria

- Demonstrates both `Pretty::new(&value)` and `inspect(&console, &value)` where appropriate.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:44:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1388","title":"demo_showcase: DemoState model","description":"Define DemoState: service statuses, pipeline steps, progress values, log ring buffer, timestamps.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:47.996627782Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:38:30.140234644Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T23:36:53.269152766Z","close_reason":"Completed: DemoState model (landed in a1b9ca7)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","state"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1388","depends_on_id":"bd-1o8x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:52:36.455149467Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1388","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:38:30.140215318Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":29,"issue_id":"bd-1388","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Data model

Keep the state small but expressive:
- run id / seed
- start time + elapsed
- services: name -> status (OK/WARN/ERR) + latency + version
- pipeline: ordered stages with progress + status + eta
- logs: ring buffer of recent lines (already formatted strings or structured records)
- current “headline” action text

## Acceptance criteria

- State can be snapshot-read without blocking (Arc<Mutex<…>> or similar).
- State updates drive visible changes in the dashboard each tick.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:41:10Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-13i9","title":"demo_showcase: Scene trait + registry","description":"Define a Scene abstraction (name/summary/run) and a registry for list/select.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrownIsland","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:05.895960587Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:12:29.479385642Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:12:29.479325871Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","scenes"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-13i9","depends_on_id":"bd-2b0s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:56.234078766Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-13i9","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:05.908657832Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":22,"issue_id":"bd-13i9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Scene API shape

Keep scenes simple and testable. Suggested shape:

- `fn name(&self) -> &'static str`
- `fn summary(&self) -> &'static str`
- `fn run(&self, console: &Arc<Console>, cfg: &Config) -> Result<(), DemoError>`

A registry returns an ordered list for full runs and a lookup map for `--scene`.

## Acceptance criteria

- `--list-scenes` prints name + summary in a nice table (itself a showcase).
- Scene ordering is stable and matches storyboard.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-164e","title":"demo_showcase: export bundle (HTML/SVG)","description":"Add optional HTML/SVG export of the demo output (capture + write files) with clear UX and paths.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:45.512659356Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:47.156286674Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","export"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-164e","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:47.156258251Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-164e","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:45.540309392Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":11,"issue_id":"bd-164e","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Scope

Support a flag that captures the demo output and writes an export bundle:
- `demo_showcase.html` (inline or external css)
- `demo_showcase.svg`

The export is an important “proof of portability”: output can be embedded in docs, GitHub issues, etc.

## UX expectations

- `--export-dir <path>` writes files there and prints a final summary panel with the paths.
- `--export` writes to a temp dir (like the existing `examples/e2e_export.rs`) and prints the dir.
- Export should be deterministic when `--width/--height` are provided.

## Acceptance criteria

- Export works with `--features full,tracing` (and without).
- The exported HTML/SVG contains representative content (not just a trivial “hello”).
- The demo prints clear instructions for viewing the artifacts.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:55Z"}]}
@@ -137,7 +137,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2smc","title":"demo_showcase: dashboard layout + live updates","description":"Build the core split-screen dashboard (Layout) and drive it with Live updates (progress/logs).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:36.694609635Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:38:49.077869634Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","layout","live"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2smc","depends_on_id":"bd-1388","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:38:49.077815984Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2smc","depends_on_id":"bd-2fl5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:30.240263939Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2smc","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:27.173009186Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2smc","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:36.713272217Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":7,"issue_id":"bd-2smc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Dashboard concept

Create a single “mission control” dashboard that stays on screen and updates live:
- **Header**: app name + run id + clock + key status badges
- **Main**: pipeline progress (multi-step), current action panel
- **Sidebar**: service status table + quick facts
- **Footer**: streaming logs (tail) + hints (“press q”, etc.)

This should be the primary wow-moment: layout + live refresh + dense styling.

## Technical approach

- Model a `DemoState` struct (progress values, current step, log ring buffer, service status, etc.).
- Use `Live::get_renderable(|| Box<dyn Renderable>)` with a closure that builds a fresh `Layout` from the current state snapshot.
- Default to `LiveOptions { screen: true, ... }` when interactive; provide flags to disable.
- Keep refresh rate modest (e.g. 10 fps max) and driven by `LiveOptions.refresh_per_second`.

## Acceptance criteria

- In an interactive terminal, the dashboard updates smoothly and exits cleanly (cursor restored; no leftover alt-screen unless `--screen`).
- In non-interactive mode, a static “snapshot” dashboard is printed once (no live loop).
- Layout adapts to narrow terminals: if width < threshold, collapse sidebar or switch to single-column mode.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:46Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2t54","title":"demo_showcase: narrow width verification","description":"Verify behavior at narrow widths (via --width) and ensure layout switches appropriately.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:45:35.223474670Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:59.903973036Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compat","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2t54","depends_on_id":"bd-3bid","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:45:35.412903908Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2t54","depends_on_id":"bd-abyl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:59.903944763Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":54,"issue_id":"bd-2t54","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## How to verify

Run with fixed width, e.g. `--width 70` and ensure:
- narrow layout mode engages
- tables/panels don't wrap into unreadable mush

## Acceptance criteria

- Narrow output still looks intentional.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:46:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2thu","title":"E2E tests: markup parsing across all renderables","description":"## Problem
The table cell markup parsing bug (where [bold #2ecc71]PLAY[/] showed literally instead of being styled) was not caught by existing tests. This indicates a gap in our testing strategy - we need E2E tests that verify markup is actually parsed and rendered correctly across ALL renderables.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create E2E tests that verify markup tags are NOT present in final rendered output
- [ ] Test markup parsing in: Table cells, Panel titles/content, Text, Tree nodes, Progress bar descriptions
- [ ] Each test should render to a string buffer and assert markup tags like [bold], [red], etc. are NOT in output
- [ ] Tests should verify styles ARE applied (check for ANSI codes or styled segments)

## Implementation Notes
- Use StringBuffer to capture output
- Pattern: render content with markup -> assert raw markup tags absent -> assert styling present
- These are integration tests, not unit tests

## Files to modify
- tests/conformance/table_tests.rs - add markup E2E tests
- tests/conformance/panel_tests.rs - add markup E2E tests  
- tests/conformance/text_tests.rs - add markup E2E tests
- May need to create new test modules","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:26.168768880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T05:43:14.690653345Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T05:43:14.690621495Z","close_reason":"Added E2E markup parsing tests across renderables; validated e2e_rendering","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
-{"id":"bd-2wxz","title":"demo_showcase: controlled failure narrative","description":"Define the ‘failure’ moment in the narrative (what fails, why) and ensure it drives useful output (logs + traceback + inspect).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:52.641139331Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:38:59.217554971Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","story"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2wxz","depends_on_id":"bd-2b0s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:07.789456633Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2wxz","depends_on_id":"bd-2xsi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:52.656509441Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":49,"issue_id":"bd-2wxz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Purpose

The “failure moment” is what makes the demo feel real: it shows how rich_rust helps when things go wrong.

## Requirements

- The failure must be believable and related to earlier output (services/pipeline/config).
- It must justify showing: logs, inspect/pretty, and traceback.
- After demonstrating the failure, the demo should recover and show a final summary (unless `--fail-fast`).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:44:44Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2xc5","title":"Fresh-eyes audit pass: renderables markdown/json/syntax","description":"Random code exploration: deep dive into markdown/json/syntax renderables, trace flow through console/render pipeline, and fix any obvious issues with tests. Ensure adherence to AGENTS.md (manual edits, no deletions).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T15:36:05.690235412Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T16:00:17.393505513Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T16:00:17.393330824Z","close_reason":"Fix list continuation indent to use cell width for bullet/marker; add regression test","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2xsi","title":"demo_showcase: observability + debugging scenes","description":"Exercise RichLogger (+ RichTracingLayer), Pretty/Inspect, and Traceback in a believable error/debug workflow.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:27.657814798Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:44.770843850Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","observability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xsi","depends_on_id":"bd-2fl5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:44.770805709Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xsi","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:42.336767127Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xsi","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:27.669647259Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":10,"issue_id":"bd-2xsi","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Story beat

After the dashboard “run” starts, introduce a controlled failure (e.g. service returns 500 / config invalid) and show how rich_rust helps debug:

- **Logging**: RichLogger formatted lines, keyword highlighting, hyperlinks to file paths
- **Tracing** (feature `tracing`): span/event demo integrated into the same output stream
- **Pretty/Inspect**: show the request/config struct being inspected
- **Traceback**: print a Rich-style exception traceback with synthetic frames

## Acceptance criteria

- Output is clearly more readable than raw logs (level colors, timestamps, structured hints).
- If `tracing` feature is not enabled, the tracing scene prints a small “disabled” message and continues.
- Traceback output is visually distinct and uses theming/styling consistent with the demo palette.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:38Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-313x","title":"demo_showcase: typography + spacing patterns","description":"Establish consistent spacing/typography patterns (rules, titles, padding) used everywhere.","notes":"Created typography.rs module with: section_header(), scene_header(), hint(), spacer(), divider(), status_badge(), status_text(), brand_accent(), muted() helpers plus SECTION_SPACING, PANEL_PADDING, BLOCK_MARGIN constants.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:05.878118586Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:41:34.395344068Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:41:34.395219205Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","typography"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-313x","depends_on_id":"bd-1by8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:19.995121744Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-313x","depends_on_id":"bd-2fl5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:05.890421007Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":28,"issue_id":"bd-313x","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Consistency rules

- Every scene starts with a styled section header (Rule + title).
- Use consistent padding around major panels.
- Prefer `Align` utilities for hero/title blocks.
- Avoid dense walls of text; break with rules, spacing, and small tables.

This bead exists to keep the final output feeling designed.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:33Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-6ozb","title":"demo_showcase: final polish + QA","description":"Polish pass: visual tweaks, timing, edge cases; ensure all quality gates pass and demo feels ‘product grade’.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:28.642592479Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:14:17.783744643Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","polish","qa"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-164e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:15.925273437Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-2175","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:08.824416694Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-2sk1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:35.835459296Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-2smc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:06.360421270Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-2xsi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:13.617768058Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-3bid","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:18.150875345Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:28.660699129Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-3rhg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:22.625349203Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-3vf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:11.348492415Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","depends_on_id":"bd-ohsq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:20.376258928Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":14,"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Checklist

- Run full quality gates:
  - `cargo fmt --check`
  - `cargo check --all-targets`
  - `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
  - `cargo test`
- Visual pass on at least:
  - wide terminal (>= 120 cols)
  - narrow terminal (~70 cols)
  - piped output (`| cat`)
- Ensure the demo ends cleanly: cursor restored, alt-screen disabled, no stray control codes.

## Acceptance criteria

- Demo looks “designed”, not like a pile of examples.
- All scenes have consistent titles, spacing, and style usage.
- Total runtime feels appropriate; `--quick` exists for CI/dev.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:37Z"},{"id":77,"issue_id":"bd-6ozb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Update: automated regression suite is required

Final QA must include the automated demo_showcase regression suite (`bd-2sk1`):
- per-scene smoke tests
- snapshot tests
- export bundle e2e tests
- non-interactive safety tests

This reduces reliance on manual eyeballing and prevents “works on my terminal” regressions.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:14:17Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-6tj5","title":"demo_showcase tests: CLI + Config unit tests","description":"Expand unit tests for CLI/config: defaults, invalid flags, and new flags like --seed/--links.","notes":"Added 12 new unit tests covering: default config values, --quick, --force-terminal, -h short form, all color system variants, missing flag values, non-finite/negative speed, all boolean pairs, width/height non-integer, positional arg rejection, and combined flags.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:04.773473520Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:46:48.320559684Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:46:48.320428780Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","demo_showcase","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6tj5","depends_on_id":"bd-2sk1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:04.794750840Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-6tj5","depends_on_id":"bd-3s5n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:16.075340749Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":67,"issue_id":"bd-6tj5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit test scope

Expand unit tests inside `src/bin/demo_showcase.rs` to cover:
- default config values (including derived defaults like interactive/live/screen auto modes)
- parsing of numeric flags (`--speed`, `--width`, `--height`, `--seed`)
- boolean flag pairs (`--foo` / `--no-foo`)
- mutual exclusion rules (e.g. `--export` vs `--export-dir`)
- friendly error messages

## Acceptance criteria

- Tests are deterministic and do not require a TTY.
- Test failures point directly to the CLI regression.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-86gp","title":"demo_showcase: capture output for export","description":"Add capture mode to demo runner so scenes can be captured to segments for exporting.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrownIsland","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:44:53.628166487Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:31:34.609990369Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:31:34.609928834Z","close_reason":"Export capture implemented: run_export() captures console output and writes HTML/SVG files. All 73 tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","export"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-86gp","depends_on_id":"bd-12nv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:41.570665666Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-86gp","depends_on_id":"bd-164e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:44:53.642928274Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-86gp","depends_on_id":"bd-1jk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:39.207097640Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":50,"issue_id":"bd-86gp","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Approach

- When `--export` or `--export-dir` is set, run the demo with capture enabled:
  - `console.begin_capture()`
  - run selected scenes
  - then call `export_html` / `export_svg`

Be careful: capture + Live/alt-screen don't mix well. For export runs, prefer non-live mode (static output).

## Acceptance criteria

- Export run produces stable artifacts without requiring a TTY.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:45:06Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-abyl","title":"demo_showcase: dashboard layout fallback (narrow)","description":"Implement a narrow terminal fallback layout (collapse sidebar / stack panels).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:52.679493487Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:37:57.320540500Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","layout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-abyl","depends_on_id":"bd-1xoj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:53:10.517989587Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-abyl","depends_on_id":"bd-2smc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:52.704817099Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":31,"issue_id":"bd-abyl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Narrow-mode behavior

When width is below a threshold (e.g. < 80):
- collapse sidebar beneath main
- reduce table columns / abbreviate labels
- prefer single-column stacking over cramped two-column layout

## Acceptance criteria

- No panics due to zero-width regions.
- Narrow mode still looks intentional and readable.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:41:25Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-eb17","title":"Port rich.prompt/status/pager equivalents (interactive helpers)","description":"Implement the interactive helper layer from Python Rich.

Scope (Rust-idiomatic):
- Prompt: styled input with validation and defaults
- Status: spinner + message context manager
- Pager: send output through pager when available

Acceptance criteria:
- Cross-platform behavior (Unix + Windows) behind crossterm
- Unit tests for parsing/validation logic; integration tests for non-interactive fallback
